mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which translates into sustained demand for goods and services, ultimately supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment typically correlates with economic strength
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE is below 5.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 5.0%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation or cooling an overheating economy. This higher cost of capital and reduced liquidity negatively impacts corporate profitability and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with a low unemployment rate indicate robust consumer spending power and confidence. This dual strength in demand and labor market health suggests a thriving economy, which directly supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong consumer spending drives economic growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative when both RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5.0% for more than 50% of observations, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals increasing manufacturing activity and business investment, reflecting strong underlying economic momentum. This expansion in the supply side of the economy translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of instances where INDPRO MoM growth exceeds 0.2%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical threshold indicates weakening demand in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns. This contraction in a key cyclical industry signals reduced future economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods when HOUST is below 1500k units, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by the CPI, erodes purchasing power and increases the likelihood of aggressive monetary policy tightening by the central bank. This environment of rising costs and potential interest rate hikes negatively impacts corporate valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to central bank tightening
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong job growth (PAYEMS) and high federal funds rates indicate an economy potentially overheating, prompting the central bank to maintain or increase restrictive monetary policy. This combination suggests a late-cycle environment where tightening financial conditions outweigh strong employment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where PAYEMS MoM growth exceeds 100k and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating GDP growth signifies a strong and expanding economy, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings. This fundamental economic strength provides a tailwind for equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of corporate profitability
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods when GDPC1 year-over-year growth is above its 5-year average, this hypothesis is rejected.
